title: linaria subalpina
intro: Linaria subalpina, also known as the Alpine Toadflax or Subalpine Toadflax, is a flowering plant species in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Linaria subalpina include its ability to soothe sore throats and calm digestive issues. Linaria subalpina can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and flowers can be used to make a tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
